title: Classic Farmhouse Kitchen Island with 4x4 Legs
summary: A farmhouse island on solid notched 4x4 legs with a 2x6 shelf and plank top — the notching technique for the stretcher joint is the one genuinely tricky step in an otherwise simple build.
description:
This island's legs are solid 4x4 posts rather than a built-up 2x4 leg assembly, which means the bottom stretcher joint needs to be notched directly into the solid post rather than pocket-holed to a face. **Set a circular saw's blade depth to 1-1/2" and make repeated cuts every 1/4" across the notch area, then clear the waste with a chisel** — this is a genuinely different technique from anything else in this catalog's islands, more carpentry than cabinetmaking, and worth practicing on a scrap 4x4 first if you haven't cut a notch this way before.

Once the notch is cut, the bottom stretcher seats into it and gets screwed and glued in place, and the top and side aprons attach with pocket holes — **drill the apron pocket holes facing upward on the inside** while you're at it, since that's what the tabletop and support boards will screw into later, and it's much easier to add these holes now than after the aprons are already installed.

The under-table supports tie into the side aprons the same way, and the shelf and tabletop are both built from 2x6 boards — genuinely thick, substantial stock that gives this island real visual weight. **Building the tabletop as its own flat panel before attaching it to the aprons** is the plan's own recommendation, following the same panel-glue-up approach used for the tops of other plank-style tables in this catalog.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Notched for the bottom stretcher with a circular saw and chisel.

steps.title: Notch the legs
steps.body: Set a circular saw's blade depth to 1-1/2". Make repeated cuts every 1/4" across the notch area on each 4x4 leg, then clear the waste with a chisel.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the bottom stretcher into the notched area with 2-1/2" screws and wood glue. Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the top end apron board and attach to the legs, checking for square. Drill pocket holes facing upward on the inside of the aprons for attaching the tabletop later. Build two leg sets.

steps.title: Attach the side aprons
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the side apron boards. Attach to the legs with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue, checking for square.

steps.title: Add the under-table supports
steps.body: Attach the under-table supports to the side aprons with pocket holes, drilling upward-facing holes for the tabletop attachment.

steps.title: Build and attach the shelf
steps.body: Build the shelf from the 2x6 boards and attach to the bottom stretchers.

steps.title: Build and attach the tabletop
steps.body: Build the tabletop as its own flat panel from the 2x6 boards, then attach to the aprons and supports.

images.alt: Farmhouse kitchen island with solid notched 4x4 legs
